theme.name: Atrium Garden
theme.setting_summary: A glass conservatory under low winter sun. The head gardener routes warm air through ducts, pollinators across the beds, and irrigation along the channels — a slow choreography that keeps everything alive past the cold months.

acts.title: Winter Planting
acts.intro_text: Light through frosted panes. Three trays of seedlings to settle, water to route, and a gardener who's been at this long enough to know the channels by heart.
acts.outro_text: By dusk the trays sit in their rows, leaves angled to whatever sun the season offers. The kettle's on. The atrium hums.

puzzles.title: First Bed
puzzles.briefing: Gardener: "Seedlings come off the propagation bench, settle them into the planter at the far end. Just a channel, that's all we need."

puzzles.title: Dividing the Beds
puzzles.briefing: Gardener: "The shipment alternates: chamomile, then thyme, then chamomile again. One bed each side of the path. Splitter at the junction."

puzzles.title: The Grafting Table
puzzles.briefing: Gardener: "Rootstock from one bench, scion from another. The grafting table joins them into something the catalogue's never seen. Reactor at the center; route the rootstock and scion in, route the graft out."

ending.good: The atrium settles into its winter rhythm. Leaves you'd given up on push out new shoots. The work asks for less of you now — the channels know where to go.
ending.neutral: The work is done, for now.
